fed_stance.label: Neutral hold
fed_stance.source: CME FedWatch for 30 Apr 2026 showed approximately 0.0% ease, 99.5% no change, and 0.5% hike, so the Fed stance remains a neutral hold.

real_yields.label: Flat
real_yields.source: FRED DFII10 showed 1.92 on 2026-04-13, versus 1.95 on 2026-04-10. The 3 bps decline remains inside the methodology's ±5 bps flat band.

usd_dxy.label: Weak/falling
usd_dxy.source: TradingView DXY was 98.132, down 0.241 (-0.24%) on the day and down 0.94% over five days, which keeps the dollar factor in weak/falling territory.

risk_mood.label: Balanced
risk_mood.source: Cboe VIX spot was 18.36, which remains inside the methodology's balanced 15-20 band rather than a high-stress regime.

vix_direction.label: Falling
vix_direction.source: Cboe VIX was down 3.97% (-0.76) from the prior close, so volatility direction is classified as falling.

growth_narrative.label: Slowing
growth_narrative.source: Atlanta Fed GDPNow remained at 1.3% for 2026:Q1, preserving a slowing U.S. growth narrative.

oil_supply_shock.label: Tightening
oil_supply_shock.source: The EIA's recent outlook still treats the Hormuz closure and related outages as key oil-market drivers, so the supply-shock classification remains tightening.

oil_inventories.label: Building
oil_inventories.source: The latest EIA weekly petroleum summary for the week ended 2026-04-03 reported a 3.1 million barrel rise in U.S. commercial crude inventories, which is a bearish inventory build.

gold_etf_flows.label: Outflowing
gold_etf_flows.source: The World Gold Council reported record March gold-ETF outflows totaling about US$12 billion, so the ETF-flow signal remains negative.

credit_spreads.label: Widening
credit_spreads.source: FRED high-yield OAS showed 2.95 on 2026-04-13, up slightly from 2.94 on the prior official reference, which classifies as widening credit spreads.

sox.label: Rising
sox.source: The latest repository-verified TradingView check showed SOX still rising on both daily and five-day horizons, so the semiconductor signal remains positive.

move_index.label: Rising
move_index.source: The latest repository-verified TradingView check showed MOVE rising on the day, so the rates-volatility factor remains negative for Nasdaq under the methodology.

yield_curve_2s10s.label: Flattening
yield_curve_2s10s.source: FRED T10Y2Y printed 0.50 on 2026-04-14 versus 0.52 on 2026-04-13, which classifies the curve as flattening on the latest official update.

copper.label: Unverified / flat
copper.source: A clean live copper source was not fully re-verified in-session, so the copper factor is conservatively scored flat rather than guessed.

ecb_stance.label: Mildly hawkish hold
ecb_stance.source: The official ECB page was not cleanly extractable in-browser, but the latest available official ECB communication and repository backdrop still support a mildly hawkish hold classification rather than an easing signal.

rate_diff_eur_usd.label: Stable
rate_diff_eur_usd.source: With the Fed neutral and the ECB on hold rather than actively easing or hiking in today's verified set, the near-term EUR-USD policy differential is best treated as stable.

eurozone_growth.label: Slowing / downgraded
eurozone_growth.source: The latest repository-verified ECB growth backdrop still reflects a downgraded euro-area outlook, so eurozone growth remains classified as slowing.

rba_stance.label: Hawkish/tightening
rba_stance.source: The RBA's 17 March 2026 decision raised the cash rate target by 25 bps to 4.10% and highlighted upside inflation risks, so the RBA stance remains hawkish.

rate_diff_aud_usd.label: Widening vs USD
rate_diff_aud_usd.source: A hawkish 4.10% RBA backdrop against a neutral Fed still leaves the AUD-USD short-rate differential supportive for AUD.

risk_sentiment_aud.label: Neutral
risk_sentiment_aud.source: The broad volatility regime is balanced rather than decisively risk-on or risk-off, so the AUD-specific sentiment factor remains neutral.

china_growth.label: Accelerating / expanding
china_growth.source: China manufacturing PMI remained above 50 in March 2026, keeping the China-growth factor in positive territory for AUD.

boj_stance.label: Neutral hold
boj_stance.source: The 19 March 2026 BoJ statement kept the overnight call rate at around 0.75%, so the safer current classification is neutral hold.

rate_diff_jpy_usd.label: Widening vs USD
rate_diff_jpy_usd.source: A neutral Fed combined with Japan's post-normalization backdrop still keeps the policy-divergence story favorable for yen futures.

geopolitical_risk.label: Elevated
geopolitical_risk.source: The EIA continues to frame Hormuz-related disruption as a live oil-market driver, so geopolitical risk for crude remains elevated.
